attacking_style: Specializes in long-range, energy-preserving moves from breakaways, typically on late uncategorized or penultimate climbs with 40km+ to go. Compensates for lack of explosive finishing speed with sustained efforts and tactical positioning.
team_tactics: Operates with significant autonomy, often bridging solo to breakaways or hunting secondary classifications. Used as decoy/experience anchor for smaller teams rather than protected leader. Minimal visible domestique support.
terrain_preference: Sustained climbs and uncategorized inclines where endurance trumps explosive power. Deliberately chooses uphill finishes to avoid descents. Excels in multi-stage KOM hunting across varied terrain rather than single steep gradients.
vulnerability: Lacks finishing speed in sprint situations from reduced groups, forcing premature attacks at flamme rouge or earlier. Poor descending explicitly acknowledged. Prone to exhausting himself in chasing/tempo-setting roles when strongest climber in break.
key_patterns: Targets KOM classification systematically from stage one through calculated point-hunting across entire races (2 races)
Attacks from breakaways on penultimate or late uncategorized climbs, not summit finishes (3 observations)
Bridges solo to established breakaways rather than initiating, preserving energy for later moves (2 observations)
Forced into early/long-range attacks due to poor finishing speed, documented at flamme rouge multiple times (2 races)
Exhausts himself when dragging breakaway groups or tempo-setting, leaving insufficient reserves for decisive move (2 observations)
form_trajectory: neutral: 2022 shows continued KOM-hunting effectiveness (dominated Dauphine by 50pts) but reduced attacking ambition, now used tactically as experienced decoy rather than stage-winning threat
